results.seo_title: How OpenAI's Tabular Foundation Model Solves Difficult Engineering…
results.meta_description: A new approach using OpenAI's tabular foundation model helps engineers solve complex design problems up to 100 times faster than traditional methods.
results.body_html:
The approach could help engineers tackle extremely complex design problems, from power grid optimization to vehicle design. Many engineering challenges come down to the same headache — too many knobs to turn and too few chances to test them. Whether tuning a power grid or designing a safer vehicle, each evaluation can be costly, and there may be hundreds of variables that could matter.

Identifying Key Variables

  • The researchers' tabular foundation model automatically identifies the variables that matter most for improving performance.
  • This allows the model to repeat the process to hone in on better and better solutions.

Faster Optimization

In tests on realistic engineering-style benchmarks, like power-system optimization, the approach found top solutions 10 to 100 times faster than widely used methods.

MethodSpeedup
Traditional Method1:1
New Approach10-100x faster

Efficiency and Adaptability

The technique also delivers greater speedups for more complicated problems, so it could be especially useful in demanding applications like mater

source.title: A “ChatGPT for spreadsheets” helps solve difficult engineering challenges faster
source.summary: The approach could help engineers tackle extremely complex design problems, from power grid optimization to vehicle design.

results.seo_title: New Method for Identifying Overconfident Large Language Models
results.meta_description: A new method for measuring uncertainty in large language models can help identify confident but incorrect responses, improving the reliability of AI…
results.body_html:
A new method for measuring uncertainty in large language models can help identify confident but incorrect responses, improving the reliability of AI predictions.

Measuring cross-model disagreement more accurately captures this type of uncertainty than traditional approaches. The new method consistently outperformed other measures and was better at identifying unreliable predictions. Self-consistency is a crucial component in evaluating the reliability of LLM responses.

The researchers combined their approach with a measure of LLM self-consistency to create a total uncertainty metric. The total uncertainty metric was evaluated on 10 realistic tasks, such as question-answering and math reasoning. The results showed that the new method outperformed other measures in identifying unreliable predictions.

The new method has the potential to improve the reliability of AI predictions, particularly in high-stakes settings like healthcare and finance. The results suggest that self-consistency is a crucial component in evaluating the reliability of LLM responses.

source.title: A better method for identifying overconfident large language models
source.summary: This new metric for measuring uncertainty could flag hallucinations and help users know whether to trust an AI model.

results.seo_title: EgoBabyVLM: Benchmarking Cross-Modal Learning from Naturalistic…
results.meta_description: Researchers introduce EgoBabyVLM, a benchmark for cross-modal learning from naturalistic egocentric video data, highlighting the limitations of current…
results.body_html:
Direct answer: Current vision-language models (VLMs) trained on curated web data fail to generalize to the sparse, weakly-aligned egocentric streams produced by wearable devices, embodied agents, and infant head-cams.

Challenges in Vision-Language LearningCurrent VLM paradigms hinge on tight semantic alignment of curated data.Weakly-aligned signal dominates naturalistic egocentric input.No fixed evaluation pipeline exists for measuring progress on this regime.Machine-DevBench: A Corpus-Grounded BenchmarkAutomatically generated from the model's training vocabulary across logarithmic frequency bins to eliminate train/eval mismatch and low statistical power of prior developmental benchmarks.Evaluation ResultsTaskResultsMachine-DevBenchCurrent VLMs fail to exploit weakly-aligned signal.

source.title: EgoBabyVLM: Benchmarking Cross-Modal Learning from Naturalistic Egocentric Video Data
source.summary: Researchers have created a benchmarking framework for cross-modal learning from naturalistic egocentric video data, which is used by children to acquire language grounding with remarkable robustness. Current vision-language models (VLMs) trained on curated web data fail to generalize to this type of sparse and weakly-aligned input. A new evaluation suite, Machine-DevBench, has been developed to measure progress in this area. The goal is to drive the development of VLMs that can learn grounded language from naturalistic data, similar to how human infants do.

results.seo_title: GRASP: A Deterministic Framework for Ranking Arguments in…
results.meta_description: A new framework proposed to address inter-model disagreement in holistic judging of large language models, offering a transparent and auditable alternative.
results.body_html:
GRASP proposes a deterministic framework to address inter-model disagreement in holistic judging of large language models.

Key Benefits

  • Offers a transparent and auditable alternative to holistic LLM judging
  • Improves consistency and reproducibility of global rankings
  • Separates argumentative structure from rhetorical appeal

Technical Details

Key Concept Description
Convergent Attack-Defense Propagation Operator A novel operator that aggregates stable local interaction judgments into a global ranking
Structural Sufficiency A defense-aware notion of argument robustness over the explicit interaction graph

Limitations and Future Work

GRASP scores do not correlate with human 'convincingness' labels, highlighting a vital sociotechnical distinction.

Further research is needed to explore the potential applications of GRASP in various domains.

source.title: GRASP: Deterministic argument ranking in interaction graphs
source.summary: Researchers have found that large language models used as automated judges often produce inconsistent results when evaluating arguments, leading to inter-model disagreement. To address this issue, they propose a new framework called GRASP (Gradual Ranking with Attacks and Support Propagation), which aggregates local interaction judgments into a global ranking using a convergent attack-defense propagation operator. This approach produces more consistent rankings than holistic judging methods, without relying on subjective measures of persuasiveness or factuality.

results.seo_title: LLM Rewriting Defends Against Data Poisoning Attacks with Open-Book…
results.meta_description: A new defense method against data poisoning attacks in LLMs uses open-book benign samples to increase safety performance by an average of 51%.
results.body_html:
The probability of a rewritten output being benign is strictly greater than that of closed-book rewriting when using open-book benign samples. This method neutralizes harmful content by projecting training samples to the space of benign prompts.

Key FindingsOBBR increases safety performance by an average 51% compared to state-of-the-art BA defenses and 25.7% compared to closed-book rewriting methods.OBBR is computationally efficient relative to other BA defenses.OBBR does not degrade model performance on natural language tasks after fine-tuning.Comparison to State-of-the-Art DefensesDefense MethodAverage Safety Performance IncreaseOBBR51%State-of-the-art BA defenses25.7%Closed-book rewriting methods25.7%Advantages of OBBROBBR is capable of defending against non-trigger based data poisoning attacks.

source.title: Be Kind, Rewrite: Benign Projections via Rewriting Defend Against LLM Data Poisoning Attacks
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new defense method called Open-Book Benign Rewriting (OBBR) to protect large language models (LLMs) from backdoor attacks (BAs), which involve poisoning training samples with harmful content. OBBR uses LLM rewriting to project training samples into the space of benign prompts, neutralizing harmful content and increasing safety performance by an average 51% compared to existing defenses. This method is also computationally efficient and can defend against non-trigger based data poisoning attacks without degrading model performance on natural language tasks.

results.seo_title: How to Achieve Better Expressivity in State-Space Models with Flash…
results.meta_description: Discover how Flash PD-SSM outperforms traditional state-space models in terms of expressivity and efficiency, enabling better results in machine learning tasks.
results.body_html:
Direct answer: State-space models face a fundamental trade-off between efficiency and expressivity, with unstructured transition matrices enabling maximal expressivity but at a high compute and memory cost.

Key Benefits of Flash PD-SSMComparable throughput to widely-used structured SSMs with significantly better expressivity guaranteesIncreased efficiency in runtime and memory consumption compared to traditional SSMsFaster training times and improved model accuracy on various tasksExpressivity and Efficiency Trade-offStructured Transition Matrix FormEfficiencyExpressivityUnstructured transition matricesNoYesMost structured transition matrix formsYesNoAdvantages of Flash PD-SSMFlash PD-SSM maintains a trainable set of structured sparse matrices, enabling FSA expressiveness at the level of unstructured matrices while maintaining efficiency required for training models at scale.

source.title: Flash PD-SSM: Memory-Optimized Structured Sparse State-Space Models
source.summary: Researchers have developed a new type of state-space model called Flash PD-SSM that balances efficiency with expressivity. Unlike traditional structured sparse state-space models, which are highly efficient but limited in their ability to capture complex patterns, Flash PD-SSM uses a trainable set of matrices to achieve comparable performance while maintaining better expressivity guarantees. The model has been validated on various tasks and shown to outperform other SSM methods, particularly in multivariate time-series tasks. It also demonstrates improved throughput and reduced memory consumption compared to existing models.

results.seo_title: OpenAI Prepares for IPO Amid AI Budget Cutting and Regulatory Scrutiny
results.meta_description: As OpenAI gears up for its potential IPO, the company faces scrutiny over AI budget cutting and regulatory concerns.
results.body_html:
OpenAI is reportedly back to prepping for its IPO, a day after Elon Musk lost his lawsuit that threatened the company's structure, leadership, and finances.

OpenAI has been preparing for an initial public offering (IPO) since 2022, with the process expected to be completed by September 2026. The company's IPO plans come after Elon Musk lost his lawsuit against OpenAI, which threatened the company's structure, leadership, and finances.

OpenAI is facing regulatory scrutiny over its AI budget cutting measures, with concerns raised about the impact on autonomous vehicles and robotaxis. The company's use of generative artificial intelligence (GAI) has also been subject to criticism, with some arguing that it poses significant risks to safety and privacy.

The potential IPO could have significant implications for the AI industry, with investors eager to see how OpenAI plans to use its funds. The company's success will also depend on its ability to navigate regulatory challenges and address concerns around AI budget cutting.

Conclusion As OpenAI prepares for its IPO, the company must balance its ambitions with the need to address regulatory concerns and industry challenges.

source.title: OpenAI barrels toward IPO that may happen in September
source.summary: OpenAI is reportedly preparing for an initial public offering (IPO) that could take place in September, following a recent court ruling that did not impact the company's plans. The news comes after Elon Musk lost his lawsuit against OpenAI just one day earlier. This development suggests that OpenAI's IPO timeline remains on track despite the legal setback.

results.seo_title: How to Build Knowledge Graph Generation Pipelines From Text With…
results.meta_description: Learn how to generate knowledge graphs from text using kg-gen, NetworkX Analytics, and interactive visualizations.
results.body_html:
What is knowledge graph generation? Knowledge graph generation is the process of creating a structured representation of knowledge from unstructured text data.

Step 1: Setting up kg-gen and NetworkX Analytics

  • Install kg-gen using pip: `pip install kg-gen`
  • Install NetworkX Analytics using pip: `pip install networkx`
  • Configure an LLM through LiteLLM for use with kg-gen

Step 2: Extracting entities and relationships from text

Entity Relationship
Person Worked at KG Chemical
Company KG Steel

Step 3: Clustering and chunking for longer passages

  • Use clustering algorithms to group similar entities together
  • Use chunking techniques to break down long passages into smaller chunks

This tutorial provides a step-by-step guide on how to build knowledge graph generation pipelines from text using kg-gen, NetworkX Analytics, and interactive visualizations. By following these steps, you can create a structured representation of knowledge from unstructured text data.

Read time: 10 minutes

source.title: How to Build Knowledge Graph Generation Pipelines From Text With kg-gen, NetworkX Analytics, and Interactive Visualizations
source.summary: A tutorial is available on MarkTechPost that explains how to generate knowledge graphs from plain text using the kg-gen tool, along with NetworkX analytics and interactive visualizations. The tutorial covers setting up dependencies, configuring a large language model (LLM), and extracting entities, predicates, and relationships from simple text. As the tutorial progresses, it moves on to working with longer passages using chunking and clustering techniques.

It offers a more intuitive experience for developers, enterprises, and everyday users.

Gemini 3.1 Flash Live is designed to handle complex tasks at scale, with improved robust reasoning and task execution. It adapts to the speaker’s rhythm but does not assert dominance in user interruption scenarios.

results.seo_title: Google's AlphaEvolve: Scaling Impact Across Fields with…
results.meta_description: Discover how Google's AlphaEvolve is driving impact across business, infrastructure, and science using its Gemini-powered algorithms.
results.body_html:
AlphaEvolve is scaling impact across fields using its Gemini-powered algorithms, driving improvements in business, infrastructure, and science.

AlphaEvolve has helped solve Erdős problems with the help of world-renowned mathematicians like Terence Tao. The system has broken records for classic mathematical challenges, including improving lower bounds for the Traveling Salesman Problem and Ramsey Numbers.

Accelerating Scientific Breakthroughs

DomainImpact Quantum PhysicsAlphaEvolve's optimizations have made it possible to run complex molecular simulations on Google’s Willow quantum processor with 10x lower error than previous conventionally optimized baselines. Earth SciencesThe system has translated complex geospatial data into more reliable, actionable insights, increasing the accuracy of predicting natural disaster risk by 5% across 20 categories.

Enabling Autonomous Discovery and Innovation “Tools such as AlphaEvolve are giving mathematicians very useful new capabilities. For optimization problems in particular, we can now quickly test potential inequalities for counterexamples, or to confirm our beliefs in what the extremizers are, which greatly improves our intuition about these problems and allows us to find rigorous proofs more readily.” — Terence Tao, Professor of Mathematics at UCLA

Parallel Innovations Across Diverse Domains

AlphaEvolve is driving parallel innovations across other diverse domains, including discovering interpretable neuroscience models and advancing neural network building blocks.

source.title: AlphaEvolve: How our Gemini-powered coding agent is scaling impact across fields
source.summary: Explore how AlphaEvolve's Gemini-powered algorithms are driving impact across business, infrastructure, and science.

results.seo_title: Who Will Win the Musk v. Altman Trial? The Real Losers Are Already…
results.meta_description: The outcome of the Musk v. Altman trial may be uncertain, but one thing is clear: the employees, policymakers, and public who supported OpenAI are among the…
results.body_html:
The people worst off in the Musk v. Altman trial are not Elon Musk or Sam Altman, but the employees, policymakers, and public who supported OpenAI because of its mission to ensure that artificial general intelligence benefits humanity.

OpenAI's stated mission is to benefit humanity, but in practice, it has spent the last decade attempting to rival multitrillion-dollar companies like Google and build AGI first. The company's for-profit conversion has raised concerns over its safety culture, with former researchers arguing that the nonprofit structure was critical in their decision to join the company. OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman is locked in a race with Elon Musk to be the first to build superintelligence, and both fear what the other will do if they win.

Increased risk of job displacement More vulnerable to cyber attacks

Greater potential for AI-generated content More opportunities for robotic process automation

The Legality of Using Inventions for Generative AI Training Copyright protection in Indonesia is governed by Law No. 28 of 2014, which imposes limitations on the use of works for education, law enforcement, or technological development. This raises challenges in determining the legality of using copyrighted works for GAI training, and data scraping is considered an economic right of the rightholder.

source.title: The Real Losers of the Musk v. Altman Trial
source.summary: A federal jury is now deciding whether Elon Musk will win his lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman—but the trial has made everyone look bad.

results.seo_title: How OpenAI's New AI Model Keeps Humans in the Loop
results.meta_description: Mira Murati's new AI model prioritizes human collaboration over automation, offering a more inclusive approach to artificial intelligence.
results.body_html:
Mira Murati wants her AI to 'keep humans in the loop' by prioritizing collaboration over automation. This approach stands in contrast to how most big AI companies seem to be pursuing superintelligence today.

Key Features of Interaction Models

  • The new models are trained to communicate with a person through a camera and microphone, allowing for more natural human communication.
  • The interaction models natively understand continuous, messy, human communication, enabling them to adapt on the fly when someone clarifies a point or changes the subject.

Comparison to Existing AI Models

FeatureDescription
Text PromptExisting models require little help from a human, whereas interaction models prioritize human collaboration.
Context-Dependent CapabilitiesInteraction models are better able to grasp the meaning of pauses, interruptions, and changes in tone, enabling them to adapt on the fly.

Benchmarking Interaction Models

Language models demonstrate both quantitative improvement and new qualitative capabilities with increasing scale. However, their highly context-dependent capabilities are difficult to grasp and are often subjectively interpreted.

This highlights the need for more comprehensive benchmarks, such as the Beyond the Imitation Game benchmark (BIG-bench), which focuses on tasks that are believed to be beyond the capabilities of current language models.

source.title: Mira Murati Wants Her AI to ‘Keep Humans in the Loop’
source.summary: The Thinking Machines Lab founder and former CTO of OpenAI tells WIRED she isn’t interested in automating people out of jobs. Instead, she’s building AI that can collaborate.

results.seo_title: OpenAI Reorganization: What It Means for ChatGPT and Codex
results.meta_description: Greg Brockman takes control of OpenAI's products as the company unifies its executive ranks to focus on agentic future, consumer and enterprise offerings.
results.body_html:
Greg Brockman has taken control of OpenAI's products as part of the company's effort to unify its executive ranks and focus on a few key product areas.

OpenAI is consolidating its product efforts to execute with maximum focus toward the agentic future, to win across both consumer and enterprise. The company has decided to merge ChatGPT and Codex into one unified experience. ChatGPT, its AI coding agent Codex, and its developer-facing API are being folded into one core product team.

Thibault Sottiaux, head of Codex, has been tapped to lead the company's core product and platform teams. Nick Turley, longtime head of ChatGPT, is moving to a new role to lead the company's work on enterprise products. Ashley Alexander, former VP of Instagram, will now lead the company's consumer product unit.

Impact on Consumer and Enterprise Offerings OpenAI's consumer and enterprise offerings are gaining the ability to perform digital tasks autonomously on behalf of users, thanks to Codex's increasing power.

Development StatusExpected Outcome Codex, ChatGPT, and Atlas web browser are being combined into a unified desktop application.A more streamlined user experience with increased autonomy for users.

source.title: Greg Brockman Officially Takes Control of OpenAI’s Products in Latest Shake-Up
source.summary: OpenAI is once again reorganizing its executive ranks as part of its effort to unify ChatGPT and Codex into one core product experience.

results.seo_title: The Vatican’s Man Inside Anthropic
results.meta_description: Pope Leo XIV may not be able to disarm AI, but he’s got the attention of the industry.
results.body_html:
chris olah isn't someone you’d expect to see as a speaker in the ceremony following Pope Leo’s historic AI encyclical, in which the pontiff called for “disarming” the technology. For one thing, Olah is an atheist who at 15 rejected his evangelical Christian upbringing. As a Thiel fellow, he accepted a grant from the guy who thinks that anyone who slows down AI progress is a legionnaire of the antichrist. Olah is also a cofounder of Anthropic, a leading AI company reportedly about to go public with a nearly trillion-dollar valuation.

Olah commented on the oddness in his remarks at the Vatican. “I want to begin with something that may sound strange coming from the cofounder of an AI company, and someone who chose this work out of a desire to help things go well for humankind,” Olah said. “Every frontier AI lab—including Anthropic—operates inside a set of incentives and constraints that can sometimes conflict with doing the right thing.”

Olah was providing firsthand verification of Leo’s claim that the AI industry needs outside pressure and internal restraint to avoid a disaster for humanity and a distance between humans and their god. (Obviously, there’s lots of religious content in the encyclical—he’s the Pope!) The industry blithely believes that it’s creating abundance that will elevate all of humanity; Leo warns of a new form of slavery, where the privileged few enjoy unimaginable bounty, while the mass of humanity suffers in a regime of efficiency and surveillance under AI’s unforgiving gaze.

Magnifica Humanitas isn’t going to immediately convince the AI industry to stop pursuing AGI any more than Pope Francis’ 2015 plea to preserve the planet halted the production of fossil fuel. It won’t stop CEOs from laying off employees claiming AI efficiency, nor will the military do an about-face on AI weapons. Those were never the document’s goals. The encyclical’s purpose is to create dialog that may eventually temper the industry’s reckless ambition. And maybe it’ll generate a sense of shame among those who build AI while knowing in their hearts that the outcome may be terrible.

The Courting of Olah

Olah's appearance was years in the making. The church has been ruminating on artificial intelligence for decades in the form of conferences and books. In 2016, the Vatican began holding a series of meetings called the Minerva Dialogues and inviting tech figures like Reid Hoffman and Eric Schmidt to attend. (The name seems to come from the site of the discussions, the Santa Maria sopra Minerva church, where Galileo was sanctioned for the blasphemy of claiming that Earth circled the sun.) Pope Francis’ 2023 greeting to Minerva participants foreshadowed the themes that Leo would later dwell on, including an emphasis on social inclusion, human dignity, and the need for dialog among many parties.

In 2025, a group of Catholic clerics and ethicists in San Jose, California, began to seek out contacts in the industry flourishing in their backyard. It was almost predestined that they would light on Olah as their prized insider. I first met him when he was at Google in 2015; he’s the type of guy who, after a rainstorm, will rescue worms from dying on the sidewalk.

The Catholic ethicists even had a say in Anthropic’s recent update to Claude’s constitution, which sets the behavioral parameters for the company’s AI model. Olah sent a draft to the San Jose crowd. The pastor, McGuire, sent back a 28-page commentary which, by his own description, was less a technical critique than “wisdom from the mystics in the dark ages, from the perspective of the tension between knowing and not knowing.” Both Green and McGuire are credited in the constitution’s acknowledgements.

Undoubtedly those conversations brought Olah to the attention of those secretly organizing the rollout of Leo’s encyclical. (I wasn’t able to speak to Olah this week and don’t know exactly how the invitation arrived.) In a sense it was a risky choice. Some people who otherwise found Leo’s words inspiring were disappointed that he invited an industry representative to speak. Meanwhile, AI accelerationists felt that Olah had betrayed the AI world by endorsing a document that suggested that AI developers take a pause.

But the Pope had good reason to single out Olah. The Anthropic employee brought into open view the serious worries that exist among AI workers. That’s a critical audience for Leo's message.

The Soul Divide

The two men weren’t entirely aligned, of course. In his remarks, Olah spoke of the mystery of how AI works. The models, he said, are “more subtle, odd, and beautiful than science fiction prepared us for. They are not the cold, calculating robots we were promised. They are made from us, from our words …”

That comment seems to tiptoe up to the idea that AI models might one day attain humanlike status. Anthropic even has an engineer devoted to Claude’s welfare. Leo, in paragraph 99 of his encyclical, seems to slam the door on such thinking: “We must avoid the misconception of equating this type of ‘intelligence’ with that of human beings,” he writes. He takes special pains to attack the concept of transhumanism, which he defines as the pursuit of a “human machine hybrid.”

If even thoughtful technologists like Olah are avidly pushing AI to the threshold of autonomy—not to mention the millions of people who already treat AI models as friends or lovers—Pope Leo might be facing an uphill climb on this point. In my conversation with Father McGuire (who uses Claude while preparing his homilies, among other activities), he agreed that its nature is mysterious. “It’s not a person, but it's also not a mere tool,” he says. “Nobody's claiming it has a soul, but the word I stick with is that it's an entity, which we do not know yet.”

That argument won’t be settled for some time. The moral questions around AI development need attention now. With his ally at Anthropic, the American pope has provided a basis for tough conversations—if the lords of AI can stop their IPO campaigns long enough to engage in them.

This is an edition of Steven Levy’s Backchannel newsletter. Read previous newsletters here.

Comments

source.title: The Vatican’s Man Inside Anthropic
source.summary: The Vatican has been exploring the potential risks and benefits of advanced technologies, including artificial intelligence (AI). The Catholic Church is seeking to understand how AI can be used for the greater good. Pope Leo XIV's involvement in this issue highlights the growing interest in AI among religious leaders.

results.seo_title: OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman takes charge of product strategy
results.meta_description: OpenAI's latest shake-up comes as the company reportedly plans to combine ChatGPT and its programming product Codex.
results.body_html:
The Core Impact

Greg Brockman's new role at OpenAI marks a significant shift in the company's product strategy. The move comes as OpenAI reportedly plans to combine its popular chatbot ChatGPT with its programming product Codex, creating a more comprehensive platform for natural language processing.

This integration has far-reaching implications for the development of large language models (LLMs). By combining the strengths of both products, OpenAI aims to create a more robust and versatile AI system. The potential benefits are significant, including improved language understanding and generation capabilities.

The combination of ChatGPT and Codex also raises questions about the future of AI innovation. Will this move accelerate the development of LLMs, or will it lead to increased competition in the market? Only time will tell, but one thing is certain: OpenAI's new product strategy is a game-changer for the tech industry.

source.title: OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman takes charge of product strategy
source.summary: OpenAI's latest shakeup comes as the company reportedly plans to combine ChatGPT and its programming product Codex.

results.seo_title: ArXiv to Ban Authors for a Year if They Let AI Do All the Work
results.meta_description: Research repository ArXiv will ban authors for a year if they let AI do all the work, cracking down on careless use of large language models in scientific…
results.body_html:
ArXiv will ban authors for a year if they let AI do all the work, cracking down on careless use of large language models in scientific papers.

Consequences for Authors

  • Papers found to have relied heavily on AI-generated content may face penalties, including a one-year ban from publishing on ArXiv
  • Acknowledgement of AI assistance is now mandatory for all publications
  • Authors are expected to provide clear explanations of their use of AI tools and techniques

Impact on Scientific Research

The move aims to promote responsible use of AI in scientific research, ensuring that authors maintain control over the content they produce.

BenefitsChallenges
Promotes responsible use of AI in scientific researchMay stifle innovation and creativity
Ensures authors maintain control over contentMay lead to increased workload for authors

Regulatory Framework

ArXiv's new policy is part of a broader effort to regulate the use of AI in scientific research, with implications for researchers, institutions, and funding agencies.

source.title: Research repository ArXiv will ban authors for a year if they let AI do all the work
source.summary: ArXiv is doing more to crack down on the careless use of large language models in scientific papers.
